title: Beam behavior
principles: Beam weapons deliver damage through maintained contact rather than conventional projectile travel.
Continuous beam application should not be described as charge behavior unless charge_time or firing_mode explicitly indicates charging.
Beam range limits where maintained contact can occur and may affect practical reach without implying poor accuracy or recoil.
Do not invent internal beam tick frequency when the application does not supply it.
Multishot, chaining, radial effects, and secondary beams may create additional instances, but their exact behavior must be explicitly supplied.
A damage ramp during continuous fire should be described as progressive damage increase while maintaining the beam, not as charging the weapon.
Beam ammunition should be described as ammunition or resource consumption, not projectile consumption.
Beam comfort depends on maintained contact, firing window, reload behavior, and supplied handling fields; high fire rate alone does not prove demanding control.
exceptions: Do not discuss projectile speed for beam delivery.
Do not infer perfect target contact from continuous delivery.
Do not assume chained or secondary beams preserve full primary-beam damage.
